title: Replace raw degradation heuristics with a causal latent wear filter
summary: Recent F1 tire-degradation work found Bayesian state-space modeling with pit-stop resets more predictive than an AR(1) benchmark.
rationale: Recent F1 tire-degradation work found Bayesian state-space modeling with pit-stop resets more predictive than an AR(1) benchmark. That matches this task: pit probability is driven by latent tire state, not just observed `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, or cumulative degradation.
implementation_hint: Create an online per-driver-race-stint filter with state reset on `PitStop`. Even a simple Kalman-style latent state can emit causal features: filtered wear level, one-step wear slope, innovation residual, and posterior uncertainty. Feed those into the existing tree pipeline instead of only hand-built window proxies.
expected_effect: Should sharpen decision boundaries around undercut/overcut windows and late-stint nonlinear deterioration.
risk: Safety-car laps, traffic, and outlier lap times can contaminate the latent state unless robust clipping or heavy-tail residual handling is added.
